slides.headline: Your track has no ISRC code?

slides.body: That's the first thing we fix at SyncMaster. Here's exactly why it matters more than almost anything else in your catalogue. ↓ HOW TO GET ONE:
ISRC = International Standard Recording Code A placement without an ISRC = money you'll never collect. Direct registration: IFPI.org

A unique identifier assigned to each individual recording.
Think of it as a barcode for your music. Cost: Free or minimal.
Time: 24–72 hours in most cases.

Why it matters for sync specifically: Through your distributor:
→ DistroKid, TuneCore, CD Baby all assign ISRCs automatically

→ It identifies your recording in global licensing databases
→ It links your track to royalty collection societies worldwide (SAMRO, COSON, SOCAN, PRS, ASCAP, and

slides.headline: Open briefs. Every Tuesday.
slides.body: Composers: apply once, get matched for life. Supervisors: shortlist in 48h.
